interactions.description: Combining warfarin (an anticoagulant) and aspirin (an antiplatelet) can synergistically increase the risk of severe internal bleeding, including gastrointestinal and intracranial hemorrhage. Monitor closely if co-prescribed.

Co-administration of sildenafil (Viagra) and nitroglycerin or other nitrates can cause a dramatic, sudden, and potentially life-threatening drop in blood pressure. This combination is strictly contraindicated.

Both lisinopril (an ACE inhibitor) and spironolactone (a potassium-sparing diuretic) promote potassium retention. Combining them can lead to elevated potassium levels (hyperkalemia), which can affect heart rhythm.

Ibuprofen can interfere with the cardioprotective antiplatelet effect of low-dose aspirin. Additionally, using both NSAIDs simultaneously increases the risk of stomach ulcers and gastrointestinal bleeding.

Amlodipine increases the systemic exposure of simvastatin. Higher simvastatin exposure increases the risk of muscle pain, weakness, or rhabdomyolysis. Simvastatin dose should be limited to 20mg daily.

Calcium ions chelate ciprofloxacin in the digestive tract, significantly reducing its absorption and efficacy. Administer ciprofloxacin at least 2 hours before or 6 hours after calcium supplements or antacids.

Combining Monoamine Oxidase Inhibitors (MAOIs) like phenelzine with SSRIs like sertraline can lead to excessive accumulation of serotonin, causing severe agitation, high blood pressure, hyperthermia, and potentially death.

Iodinated contrast media can cause temporary kidney impairment, leading to accumulation of metformin and an increased risk of metformin-associated lactic acidosis. Metformin should be temporarily discontinued prior to or at the time of the procedure.
